summary: This cluster of research focused on the phylogenetic and biochemical characterization of poly-hydroxybutyrate (PHB) as a cellular inclusion in blue-green algae (cyanobacteria). The studies confirmed the presence of PHB in seven species, including *Chlorogloea fritschii*, *Nostoc commune*, and *Oscillatoria decolarata*, with the first documented instance of this compound in *Chlorogloea fritschii*. Using fluorescent staining with Nile blue, researchers identified PHB as a lipid-based energy and carbon storage material, highlighting its potential role in metabolic adaptation to environmental stressors. The findings also provided insights into the taxonomic classification of blue-green algae through metabolic evolution, though the geographic or ecological scope of the sampled species was not specified.
entity_sample: observed with NIKON OPTIPHOT with fluorescence attachments at 460 nm
